jokes.text: Why did they cancel Futurama?
jokes.setup: Kent reporting live from scenic tourist location
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to lament cancellation of creator's other show
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Matt Groening's other show being cancelled, delivered deadpan during news report. Self-referential humor that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: You crushed my boyfriend! You better be good at making out!
jokes.setup: Geezer Rock falls and crushes someone
jokes.punchline: Immediate replacement demand with oddly specific criteria
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combining tragedy with pragmatic teen priorities. Good economy and unexpected perspective.

jokes.text: What's the big deal about a rock that looks like a dude? I've got a dad that looks like a monkey.
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual comparison of Homer to a monkey
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart irreverence toward Homer. Well-integrated character humor but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Per our agreement!
jokes.setup: Bart promised to stop comparing Homer to a monkey
jokes.punchline: Homer's formal invocation of their deal
jokes.explanation: Homer's unexpectedly formal language contrasts with the silly subject matter. Good economy.

jokes.text: Knock-kneed home renters. Can't we thin this herd with some smallpox-infected blankets?
jokes.punchline: Genocidal solution to tourist annoyance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character integration - casual historical genocide reference. Dark humor that's shocking but earned through character.

jokes.text: We're already immune, you jerk!
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact immunity claim
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic treating historical genocide as casual medical concern. Good callback to Burns' suggestion.

jokes.text: There's a lot you don't know about me, Marge. A lot that would shock you.
jokes.setup: Marge points out Homer doesn't sell roadside corn
jokes.punchline: Homer's mysterious implication about hidden depths
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional self-importance about mundane activities. Good character integration but predictable.

jokes.text: Thank you, Blood and Tears. Sorry to hear about Sweat.
jokes.punchline: Reference to missing member of Blood, Sweat & Tears
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay on classic band name. Efficient delivery but lacks character integration.

jokes.text: Now, to do something I've never done before. Help out an old man.
jokes.setup: Burns preparing to escape falling rocks
jokes.punchline: Ironic claim about helping others when saving himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns selfishness disguised as altruism. Good character consistency and ironic structure.

jokes.text: Here's a poem. Run fast or lose your ass!
jokes.setup: Lisa disappointed about not reading her poem
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude rhyming emergency advice
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at poetry under pressure. Good structure with rhyme and urgency, very quotable.

jokes.text: And tonight was the night I was gonna show him my tattoo.
jokes.punchline: Revealing intimate plan with his boss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers character moment revealing his devotion. Dark humor about inappropriate workplace relationship.

jokes.text: I'd be a publisher, just like Katharine Graham! Or that lady who wheels Larry Flynt around.
jokes.punchline: Comparing respected journalist to porn publisher's assistant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of high and low journalism figures. Shows Lisa's knowledge with inappropriate comparison.

jokes.text: That stays in.
jokes.punchline: Deciding to include his mistake in the recording
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about recording/editing process. Burns' ego prevents him from removing mistakes.

jokes.text: Because of my svelte physique, I was able to fit in a narrow air pocket.
jokes.punchline: Burns describing his frail body as 'svelte'
jokes.explanation: Burns' vanity turning weakness into positive trait. Good character consistency with his delusions.

jokes.text: Thankfully, a mother mole nursed me as her own until I was strong enough to continue.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous image of Burns being nursed by mole
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd survival story. Creates memorable visual and sets up later callback. High originality.

jokes.text: I have been 89 for a while now.
jokes.setup: News report saying rock was thousands of years old
jokes.punchline: Burns relating to ancient age
jokes.explanation: Burns acknowledging his extreme age. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Thank you, Geezer Rock, for doing what none of us had the courage to do, smush Mr. Burns.
jokes.punchline: Celebrating Burns' apparent death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about public hatred for Burns. Good structure with formal thanks for violence.

jokes.text: This town cared more for a soulless piece of granite than for me.
jokes.setup: Burns hearing how little he's missed
jokes.punchline: Ironic complaint given his own soullessness
jokes.explanation: Ironic self-pity from actually soulless character. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Don't be so needy!
jokes.punchline: Burns rejecting support while seeking love from town
jokes.explanation: Burns' hypocrisy about neediness. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You eliminated that program in the '30s, sir. They were getting too grabby!
jokes.setup: Burns asking about turkey program for poor
jokes.punchline: Cancelling charity because recipients wanted more
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic - blaming poor for being poor. Dark humor with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Bring me a checkbook and a mother mole!
jokes.punchline: Casual request for mole alongside business tool
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to mole nursing with perfect economy. Absurd juxtaposition with business needs.

jokes.text: Man driving erratically throws papers out window!
jokes.punchline: Describing their paper delivery as dangerous driving
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious self-description. Good character integration showing his lack of awareness.

jokes.text: I haven't cried like this since the third Mr. Teeny died.
jokes.punchline: Reference to multiple monkey deaths
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about show business animal mortality. Perfect Krusty character moment with morbid specificity.

jokes.text: You couldn't hold a candle to him. Neither could you!
jokes.punchline: Extending insult to current monkey then himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Krusty's self-deprecation. Character-appropriate sentimentality about animals.

jokes.text: Perfect. You're our TV critic.
jokes.setup: Nelson saying he makes nerds cry
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately connecting this to TV criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant connection between bullying and harsh criticism. Shows Lisa's quick wit and satirizes media criticism.

jokes.text: I don't know. Making nerds cry? Perfect. You're our TV critic.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about Nelson's journalism skills
jokes.punchline: His bullying qualifies him for TV criticism
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on TV criticism as organized bullying. Perfect character integration for Nelson.

jokes.text: I want to be a fire truck. How about a feature columnist? Yeah, I'm a feature columnist!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Ralph about journalism role
jokes.punchline: Ralph's non-sequitur then immediate agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph dialogue - completely random then enthusiastic acceptance. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Burns is alive? Then whose skull am I drinking beer out of?
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of drinking from human skull
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor with perfect Barney delivery. Shocking revelation treated casually. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Whose grave? Uh, the unknown soldier? Carry on.
jokes.setup: Others questioning whose grave Barney will dance on
jokes.punchline: Randomly picking patriotic target then awkward dismissal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about disrespecting military honors. Good awkward recovery from Barney.

jokes.text: Want to split an ice cream sundae? Done and done!
jokes.setup: Burns increasingly large bribes all rejected
jokes.punchline: Tiny offer of shared dessert succeeds
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - huge bribes fail, tiny gesture succeeds. Excellent economy and surprise.

jokes.text: Snot a problem, chief. Don't call me chief. Sure thing, jerk. Chief is fine.
jokes.punchline: Bart's pun then negotiating terms of address
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'snot' plus sibling power dynamic negotiation. Solid character interaction.

jokes.text: No support for the naughty hotties!
jokes.setup: Burns complaining about pretty girls avoiding tickets
jokes.punchline: Radio hosts' crude summary of his point
jokes.explanation: Radio shock jock style reduction of Burns' complaint. Good character voice for morning DJs.

jokes.text: Indoor plumbing. The lack of it killed my mother.
jokes.setup: Burns enjoying toilet flush sound effect
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about mother's death
jokes.explanation: Dark Burns family history delivered casually. Good contrast between modern convenience and personal tragedy.

jokes.text: Oh, Cletus. Tonight, you can knock me up again.
jokes.punchline: Crude pregnancy reference as romantic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice for hillbilly characters. Crude but appropriate to their established personalities.

jokes.text: Television, jumping box, picto-cube, just crank it up!
jokes.punchline: Multiple old-fashioned names for TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns old-fashioned language. Creative made-up terms showing his age and disconnect.

jokes.text: He's got a heart as big as my boobs!
jokes.punchline: Comparing his character to her prominent physical feature
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crude comparison. Good economy using her defining physical trait as metaphor.

jokes.text: Don't end up like me. Vote Republican. God bless America. This cartoon was made in Korea.
jokes.punchline: Ironic patriotic message followed by foreign production credit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about animation outsourcing. Perfect structure building to ironic revelation.

jokes.text: stinky pants.
jokes.setup: Lisa's serious editorial about Burns' monopoly
jokes.punchline: Ending serious critique with childish insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - sophisticated analysis ending with kid-like insult. Shows her age despite intelligence.

jokes.text: No! No! I won't take your blood ponies!
jokes.setup: Burns offering beautiful ponies as bribe
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dramatic rejection with ominous description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phrase 'blood ponies' - makes innocent bribe sound sinister. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I can't! My mom's not picking me up for an hour!
jokes.punchline: Practical childhood constraint on dramatic exit
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with realistic kid problem. Brilliant character work showing Lisa's age.

jokes.text: My God, are you always on?
jokes.setup: Lisa giving serious answer about music and social issues
jokes.punchline: Burns exasperated by her constant activism
jokes.explanation: Burns perfectly capturing Lisa's exhausting righteousness. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: And by the way, that story I filed from Baghdad was all made up. I was actually in Basra.
jokes.punchline: Confession of journalistic fraud with meaningless distinction
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of war reporting fraud. The minor geographic correction makes confession absurd.

jokes.text: the pen is mightier than the flaming bag of poop.
jokes.punchline: Comparing writing to his preferred prank method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart-ification of famous quote. Perfect character integration with his prank history.

jokes.text: I can't be held responsible for what my goons were ordered to do!
jokes.setup: Smithers mentioning Burns' goons attacked Lisa
jokes.punchline: Burns claiming innocence while admitting he ordered it
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction revealing Burns' twisted thinking. Good structure with self-defeating argument.

jokes.text: That was totally worth it.
jokes.setup: Power cut stops Spice Girls song mid-play
jokes.punchline: Bart's satisfaction at the interruption
jokes.explanation: Good Bart reaction to avoiding bad music. Character-appropriate priorities and good economy.

jokes.text: That smell takes me back.
jokes.setup: Skinner showing Lisa the mimeograph machine
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reaction to machine smell (sets up Vietnam flashback)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Skinner's Vietnam history. Character-appropriate nostalgia with ominous undertone.

jokes.text: It wasn't quite the same.
jokes.setup: Skinner reunited with his chair after war
jokes.punchline: Understated disappointment about war trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement about PTSD. Perfect Skinner character voice making light of serious trauma.

jokes.text: My league! My beautiful league! All dead.
jokes.setup: Burns calling on League of Evil
jokes.punchline: Discovering they've all died
jokes.explanation: Burns' sentimental reaction to evil colleagues' deaths. Good character moment showing his isolation.

jokes.text: Have some congratulatory drugs.
jokes.punchline: Casual offer of illegal substances
jokes.explanation: Burns treating drugs as casual reward. Good economy and character-appropriate criminality.

jokes.text: Have some congratulatory drugs.
jokes.setup: Burns praising Homer for seeing through his scheme
jokes.punchline: Casually offering drugs as reward
jokes.explanation: Casual drug offering shows Burns' corruption. Good character moment and economy.

jokes.text: Don't you love it when nerds don't know they're nerds?
jokes.punchline: Burns' cruel enjoyment of self-delusion
jokes.explanation: Burns finding cruel pleasure in others' lack of self-awareness. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Hello, Beefcake Charlie.
jokes.punchline: His own delusional self-image
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment - Burns exhibiting the exact delusion he was mocking. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Put on a pot of coffee, drink it and start making burgers!
jokes.setup: Homer working all night for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Demanding multiple forms of support from Marge
jokes.explanation: Homer's demanding approach to self-sacrifice. Good escalation and character-appropriate selfishness.

jokes.text: except still a dude!
jokes.setup: Homer praising Lisa in his newspaper
jokes.punchline: Important caveat about gender
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer clarification showing his masculine insecurity even while praising daughter.

jokes.text: I reviewed the new tractors. They're all shite!
jokes.punchline: Agricultural criticism with signature accent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie voice and perspective. Character-appropriate subject matter and delivery.

jokes.text: now there's a thousand freaks xeroxing their worthless opinions.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's harsh assessment of citizen journalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant media satire that predicted social media era. Sharp observation with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Well, blow me down. I'm a Selma.
jokes.setup: Quiz about Patty vs Selma personality types
jokes.punchline: Moe's surprised self-discovery
jokes.explanation: Good Moe reaction to personality quiz. Character-appropriate surprise at self-knowledge.

jokes.text: Unless of course you're Rupert Murdoch. He is one beautiful man.
jokes.punchline: Exception for media mogul with weird compliment
jokes.explanation: Sharp media criticism with absurd personal compliment. Good satirical observation about media consolidation.
